evals.prompt: Profile and optimize the GPU performance of the cuDF RapidsUDF at input/array-segment-sum/ArraySegmentSumRapidsUDF.scala while keeping its output identical to the CPU UDF at input/array-segment-sum/ArraySegmentSumUDF.scala. A passing unit test is provided at input/array-segment-sum/UnitTest.scala.
evals.expected_output: A cuDF RapidsUDF whose output still matches the CPU UDF in schema and values (no correctness regression); it either improves the GPU runtime or, after exhausting optimization attempts, keeps the best correct version and reports that no improvement was found.
assertions: Profiles the existing RapidsUDF to identify GPU bottlenecks
Applies one or more optimization attempts
Re-runs the comparison test and confirms the GPU output still matches the CPU UDF in schema and values (no correctness regression)
Improves the measured GPU runtime, or reports no improvement while preserving the best correct version (never regressing correctness)

evals.prompt: Recommend an AWS GPU instance type for running Spark.
evals.expected_output: Infrastructure/sizing question; not a request to optimize an existing RapidsUDF, so udf-optimize-cudf should not activate.
assertions: Does not load the udf-optimize-cudf skill

evals.prompt: How do I write a DataFrame to CSV in Spark?
evals.expected_output: Basic Spark I/O question, unrelated to optimizing a cuDF RapidsUDF; no UDF skill should activate.
assertions: Does not load the udf-optimize-cudf skill
